2002-06-01.title: Text of Bush's Speech at West Point
2002-06-01.abstract: Following is the full text from President Bush's commencement address at the United States Military Academy at West Point Saturday, as recorded by The New York Times.

2002-06-01.title: World Briefing | Asia: Taiwan: Officials Quit After Air Crash
2002-06-01.abstract: All nine senior officials at the China Aviation Development Foundation, a quasi-governmental group that owns 71 percent of China Airlines, resigned to take responsibility for the crash last Saturday of a China Airlines Boeing 747 into the Taiwan Strait. all 225 aboard died. Rough seas and powerful currents have prevented the recovery of the aircraft's data and cockpit voice recorders and most of the wreckage.   Keith Bradsher (NYT)

2002-06-01.title: Kashmiri Impelled to Lead by His Father's Death
2002-06-01.abstract: Profile of Umar Farooq, Kashmiri Muslim political and spiritual leader, who seeks political settlement of Kashmir crisis; assumed roles from father, assasinated in 1990, and moderate separatist leader Abdul Ghani Lone, assassinated this May; is possible target of Kashmiri militants backed by Pakistani intelligence agency, who favor violent route to independence; Indian government, unwilling to negotiate Kashmir's status, will not talk to him; photo (M)

2002-06-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Yugoslavia: A Nation Reshaped
2002-06-01.abstract: Parliament overwhelmingly endorsed a looser union between the two remaining republics. Constitutional experts now have to work out the exact structure of the new state, to be called Serbia and Montenegro, which was created under pressure from the European Union to stave off a Montenegrin independence bid and to discourage other Balkan separatists, especially in Kosovo, a province of Serbia. Serbia and Montenegro will be broadly autonomous but share a president, cabinet and army; either can leave the union after three years.   Daniel Simpson (NYT)

2002-06-01.title: New U.S. Commander At Helm in Afghanistan
2002-06-01.abstract: Lt Gen Dan K McNeill formally assumes command of American-led forces in Afghanistan; says first goal is to put in place more systematic effort to hunt and kill elusive Qaeda and Taliban forces, interview; says he will not use coalition troops to provide nationwide security; photo (M)

2002-06-02.title: U.S. MUST ACT FIRST TO BATTLE TERROR, BUSH TELLS CADETS
2002-06-02.abstract: Pres Bush delivers toughly worded speech to 1,000 graduates at United States Military Academy that seems aimed at preparing Americans for potential war with Iraq; says cold war doctrines of containment and deterrence are irrelevant in world where only strategy for defeating America's new enemies is to strike them first; says to wait for threats to fully materialize is to wait too long; says enemy's plans must be disrupted and worst threats be confronted before they emerge; he does not mention Iraq by name, but says 'even weak states' could hope to 'strike great nations'; reminds graduates that in their last year at West Point America was attacked by 'ruthless and resourceful enemy'; says they are graduating in time of war; photo (M)

2002-06-02.title: Key Palestinian Security Posts Becoming Harder to Fill
2002-06-02.abstract: Yasir Arafat and his top aides are scrambling to address rising Palestinian and international demands for reform of his multiple security forces; but pressure from Bush administration and elsewhere may be having unintended consequences; some top officials whom Israelis regard as possible successors to Yasir Arafat are shying away from security jobs, fearing that such posts could make them appear to be doing Israelis' bidding; Prime Min Ariel Sharon says thorough changes to Palestinian Authority are condition for his return to peace negotiations; Bush administration favors political talks alongside reform; because there has been no public demand for talks by Bush administration, Palestinians fear that even its talk of reform is stalling tactic; photos (M)

2002-06-02.title: On Kashmir Line, Pakistanis Wait, With Few Illusions
2002-06-02.abstract: Soldiers on Pakistani side of Ksahmir's disputed frontier wait for assault by Indian Army that they expect could come at any moment; they harbor few illusions about their chances against their more powerful enemy; they are plotting defense and their military leaders are defiant; this reflects belief among Pakistanis that it would be Indians who start war, but that once one started, Pakistanis, however outgunned and outnumbered, would not suffer humiliations visited on them in wars past; world leaders fear that India's overwhelming superiority in conventional forces could prompt desperate Pakistani leadership to use their nuclear weapons to halt attack; photo (M)

2002-06-02.title: Africa Creeps Along Path to Democracy
2002-06-02.abstract: Democracy is slowly, imperfectly, inching across Africa, continent better known for wars, disease, corruption and repression; since 1990, 42 of 48 countries in sub-Saharan Africa have held multiparty elections; in early 1980's, only four countries held multiparty elections on continent dominated by military juntas and autocrats; one dictator, Mobutu Sese Seko of Zaire, famously declared that 'democracy is not for Africa'; today, despite common complaints of vote rigging and political intimidation, most Africans have right to choose their leaders at ballot box; over last two years, governing parties in Senegal, Mauritius, Ghana and Mali have peacefully handed power to opposition party rivals; but for many ordinary Africans, tangible benefits of political change remain elusive; so far, democracy has often been followed by period of deteriorating living standards, and many Africans are now freer but poorer than they have been in decades; long economic decline is linked to poor leadership, falling demand for Africa's commodities and reluctance of Western governments to open their markets to African products; foreign aid to Africa, which soared during cold war as Soviet Union and United States competed for allies among continent's dictators, has dropped to its lowest levels; photos; graph (L)

2002-06-02.title: Flora Lewis, Astute Observer of World Affairs for The Times and Others, Dies at 79
2002-06-02.abstract: Flora Lewis, New York Times foreign affairs columnist, dies at age 79; photo (M)

2002-06-03.title: 16 Arrested in Killings of 26 Over Land Disputes in Mexico
2002-06-03.abstract: Fifteen men and woman are jailed in Oaxaca, Mexico, on suspicion of killing 26 villagers from rural reaches of Oaxaca State in vendetta over land; officials say suspects were residents of nearby villages that have fought people of Santiago Xochiltepec, where victims were from, for many years; maps; phooto (S)

2002-06-03.title: Flora Lewis, 79, Dies; Keen Observer of World Affairs
2002-06-03.abstract: Flora Lewis, New York Times foreign affairs columnist, dies at age 79; photo (M)

2002-06-03.title: Rights Lawyer's Odd Death Tests Mexican Justice
2002-06-03.abstract: Focus on October 2001 murder of leading Mexican human rights crusader Digna Ochoa and its implications for Mexico's justice system and Pres Vicente Fox, who has pledged to strengthen rule of law in nation that became democracy with his election in 2000; government investigators are preparing to declare death suicide, citing forensic evidence and trail of correspondence; move sets off alarm among human rights activists in Mexico and abroad, who accuse prosecutors of blaming victim no longer able to defend herself rather investigate soldiers, paramilitary groups and corrupt rural political bosses who were targets of her work; photos (M)

2002-06-03.title: Senegal stuns France, 1-0, as World Cup opens
2002-06-03.abstract: SEOUL:—  Sport is able to conjure magic. Here in the capital of South Korea on Friday, Senegal, at its first World Cup, beat the champion of the world and Senegal's former colonial master, France, 1-0. The solitary goal was scored by a big midfielder, Pape Bouba Diop.

2002-06-03.title: War Is Already a Reality For Villagers in Kashmir
2002-06-03.abstract: War is already reality for many Kashmir villagers who live near border with India and have been forced to flee homes because of rising tensions over disputed area; some comment; photo (M)

2002-06-04.title: World Briefing | Asia: The Hague: Indonesia-Malaysia Island Case
2002-06-04.abstract: Indonesia is taking Malaysia before International Court of Justice over two scenic islands, Sipadan and Litigan, which Indonesia claims; Indonesia claims it was accorded islands by 1891 Anglo-Dutch treaty between region's colonial powers and says Malaysia erred by printing maps showing islands as its territory; map (S)

2002-06-04.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: Talks On A Rights Tribunal
2002-06-04.abstract: United Nations human rights envoy to Cambodia Peter Leuprecht says number of countries are pressing UN and Cambodia to resume talks on holding international trial for surviving Khmer Rouge leaders; UN recently withdrew from talks saying Cambodian side did not seem serious about reaching agreement; this is first suggestion that talks could resume (S)

2002-06-04.title: Wolfowitz, in Philippines, Looks to a Greater U.S. Role
2002-06-04.abstract: Deputy Defense Sec Paul D Wolfowitz arrives, Tabiawan, Philippines, to assess plans for American troops to extend counterterrorism training mission with Philippine military and to become more involved in searching for Gracia and Martin Burnham, Americans held by militant Muslim group Abu Sayyaf; meets with American and Philippine commanders and community leaders on Basilan Island; maps; photo (M)

2002-06-04.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-06-04.abstract: SWITZERLAND: Bishops Deplore Abortion Vote ; GERMANY: Leader To Boycott Forum ; Northern IRELAND: New Belfast Violence.

2002-06-04.title: Jacques Fauvet, 87, Journalist And Ex-Director of Le Monde
2002-06-04.abstract: Jacques Fauvet, former Le Monde director general, dies at age 87; photo (M)

2002-06-05.title: Hong Kong Passions About Tiananmen Seem to Be Fading
2002-06-05.abstract: Hong Kong annual candlelight vigil to remember protesters killed by Chinese military in Beijing's Tiananmen Square in 1989 draws sparser crowd than usual this year; poll by group with reputation for impartiality finds that more than half of Hong Kong's population is satisfied with Beijing's rule of China and, separately, with Beijing's handling of Hong Kong affairs (M)

2002-06-05.title: Rare Iguanas in Galapagos Are Victims of Oil Spill
2002-06-05.abstract: Small amounts of oil caused a big die-off of the algae-eating lizards in the months following a spill.

2002-06-05.title: World Briefing | Pacific: Samoa: New Zealand Regrets Abuses
2002-06-05.abstract: Prime Min Helen Clark of New Zealand, former semicolonial ruler of Western Somoa, visits Apia to apologize for New Zealand's negligence in influenza epidemic in 1918, which killed 8,000 islanders, and police suppression in 1929 of independence movement and later banishment of island chiefs; photo (S)

2002-06-05.title: Son of South Korea's President Indicted
2002-06-05.abstract: Kim Hong Gul, the youngest of President Kim Dae Jung's three sons, was indicted today on charges of bribery and influence peddling.

2002-06-05.title: French Far Right Makes Headway in Battle for Left's Onetime Northern Stronghold
2002-06-05.abstract: Jean-Marie Le Pen's far-right National Front party is anticipating gains against Socialists and Communists in parliamentary elections in France's north, where unemployment is twice the national average; some Socialists admit they have lost touch with their blue-collar power base; map; photo of Marine Le Pen, Le Pen's daughter, campaigning for National Assembly seat in Lens, onetime coal town (M)

2002-06-06.title: Iraq Is Said to Step Up Attacks on Allied Jets
2002-06-06.abstract: British defense secretary Geoff Hoon reports worrisome increase in Iraq's efforts to shoot down American and British warplanes patrolling two no-flight zones over its territory, after significant lull immediately after Sept 11; declines to say if attempts to shoot down allied warplanes are being matched by any other kind of war footing in Iraq; emphasizes importance of allowing United Nations weapons inspectors back into Iraq with freedom to roam nation; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld has expressed skepticism about ability of international arms monitors to puncture disinformation, decoys and denials from Pres Saddam Hussein (M)

2002-06-06.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-06-06.abstract: MYANMAR: 9 Opposition Members Freed ; CHINA: Death Sentence For Cover-up

2002-06-06.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-06-06.abstract: Northern IRELAND: A Sinn Fein Lord Mayor ; IRELAND: A New Opposition Leader ; YUGOSLAVIA: Amnesty For Albanian Rebels ; MACEDONIA: Ex-guerrillas Enter Politics ; Czech REPUBLIC: Ex-mayor To Fight Corruption.

2002-06-06.title: Half the Afghans, the Women, Fight to Establish Their Rights
2002-06-06.abstract: With Afghanistan's grand council set to convene next week, there has never been a moment with more promise — or long-term danger — for Afghan women.

2002-06-06.title: A Defiant Arafat Emerges From Compound After Israeli Attack
2002-06-06.abstract: Israeli troops charged into Yasir Arafat's compound today leaving the Palestinian leader's position more shaky than ever.

2002-06-07.title: French Politics Finds Little Room for Women
2002-06-07.abstract: France's two-stage parliamentary vote starts June 9; when completed, women's share of seats is unlikely to exceed the 55 of 555 seats they gained in last parliamentary election, in 1997, despite law passed in 2000 that called on political parties to ensure that 50 percent of all election candidates be women; politically active women note that France was among last European nations to give women the vote, in 1944; say women who are picked as candidates are frequently sent to contest districts where their parties have little or no chance of success; photo (M)

2002-06-07.title: Half the Afghans, the Women, Fight to Establish Their Rights
2002-06-07.abstract: Three Afghan women arrive in New York for meetings with American foundations, law firms and Bar Assn of City of New York; they are seeking help because, they say, newly won women's freedoms in Afghanistan are already under threat; say women must take part in writing country's Constitution if they are to achieve protection; three women belong to Afghan Women Lawyers and Professionals Assn, which emerged in Jan from four years underground; group has trained 40 women in basic human rights and wants to send dozens more across Afghanistan to explain to women in towns and rural villages about evolution of political life and how they can work to ensure that developments work to their advantage; Afghanistan's ground council convenes next week and it will select writers of new Constitution (M)

2002-06-07.title: White House Is Working To Nail Down Mideast Plan
2002-06-07.abstract: Latest suicide bombing and Israeli reprisal raids elicit strong and familiar condemnations from senior Bush administration officials, but they also insist that events will not deter them from discussing new proposals for peace with Pres Hosni Mubarak of Egypt this weekend and Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel when he arrives day later; Bush and his national security adviser Condoleezza Rice are said to be leaning toward notion of outlining parameters or timetable for final peace settlement that would follow lines of proposals under discussion before negotiations broke down; international pressure for presidential response expected to intensify after Bush's meetings with Mubarak and Sharon (M)

2002-06-07.title: Refugees Languishing in Camps as the U.S. Admits Many Fewer
2002-06-07.abstract: Refugee resettlement groups say that because of security concerns, refugee admissions to US since Sept 11 have slowed to trickle, even as number of civilians fleeing war worldwide inches up; says result is refugees languishing in crowded, foul and sometimes unsafe camps overseas; Bush administration set at 70,000 number of refugees it would admit in current fiscal year, which began last Oct 1; so far, fewer than 14,000 have been allowed to enter (M)

2002-06-07.title: Small German Party Tries to Calm Uproar Over Slurs About Jews
2002-06-07.abstract: Germany's Free Democratic Party leader Guido Westerwelle engineers resignation of Jamal Karsli, party politician who compared Israel's tactics in West Bank to 'Nazi methods'; Westerwelle does not fire his deputy Jurgen Mollemann, who had recruited Karsli and who most offended Germany's Jewish Organizations when he said that Jewish leader Michel Friedman inspired anti-Semitism by his behavior; rivals to Free Democrats, party hoping to gain balance of power after Sept 22 national elections, charge Westerwelle and Mollemann with cynical politicking among right wing in Germany by playing anti-Israel and anti-Semitic themes (M)

2002-06-08.title: World Briefing | Europe: France: Accord Against Bioweapons
2002-06-08.abstract: Informal grouping of 33 countries from Europe, Latin America and Pacific meet in Paris and urge new measures to prevent spread of chemical and biological weapons (S)

2002-06-08.title: On Eve of Sharon's U.S. Visit, 2 Settlers Are Killed in Raid
2002-06-08.abstract: At least three people die, including attacker, and four more are wounded when two Palestinian gunmen attack heavily guarded Karmei Tzur Jewish settlement in West Bank; attack comes on eve of US visit by Prime Min Ariel Sharon and day after Israeli forces swept into Palestinian-controlled towns in search of wanted militants; Israeli government steps up diplomatic and public relations offensive against Yasir Arafat; charge him with failing to stop terrorist attacks; Arafat urges Bush administration to move quickly toward international peace conference for Middle East (M)

2002-06-08.title: A Painful Consequence Of Political Constraints
2002-06-08.abstract: News analysis of death of American missionary Martin Burnham during rescue attempts by Filipino forces after kidnapping by Abu Sayyaf rebels more than year ago; American military experts and lawmakers question whether outcome may have been different if mission involved American soldiers, who trained Filipino rangers but were not allowed to play direct combat role in rescue; incident illustrates limitations on US military might and painful trade-offs required to keep important allies in global campaign against terrorism; photo (M)

2002-06-08.title: Sentenced to Death, Rape Victim is Freed by Pakistani Court
2002-06-08.abstract: Pakistani court bows to public outrage and frees Zafran Bibi, rape victim sentenced to death by stoning for crime of having extramarital relations; she leaves prison in Islamabad with seven-month-old daughter conceived when husband was in prison and described by judge as proof of her crime; was prosecuted after accusing brother-in-law of raping her; human rights groups say as many as half of women who report rape are charged with breaking Islamic law, which forbids any sexual contact outside of marriage; photo (M)

2002-06-08.title: Gays in Jerusalem Parade Their Pride
2002-06-08.abstract: First gay pride parade is held in Jerusalem over loud protests from Orthodox Jewish politicians and some demonstrators, who condemn it as celebration of sin; photo (M)

2002-06-09.title: U.N. Official Urges Food Aid For the Poor As a Priority
2002-06-09.abstract: Jacques Diouf, head of United Nations Food and Agriculture Orgn, says development experts mesmerized by international trade and high technology must tackle poverty first; says 815 million people go to sleep hungry, and when officials from 183 nations meet in Rome June 10, they should reassess world food situation; his organization estimates that poor countries are falling $30 billion a year short of $180 billion needed to meet targets for cutting hunger that were set at world food summit meeting in 1996 (M)

2002-06-09.title: Bush Urges Immediate Work on Palestinian State
2002-06-09.abstract: Pres Bush says United States must start working immediately with Israelis and Palestinians toward establishing Palestinian state in Middle East, but that he is not prepared to recommend timetable for state's creation; makes remarks in joint news conference at Camp David with Pres Hosni Mubarak of Egypt, who in his opening statement calls for 'agreed time frame' leading to establishment of state; Bush, who will meet at White House with Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel on June 10, says institutions necessary for emergence of Palestinian state must be built before deadline for state could be set; Bush and Mubarak offer differing views of Yasir Arafat, with Mubarak saying he should be given chance to reform Palestinian Authority, and Bush speaking of need to reach out to newer Palestinian leaders; photo (M)

2002-06-09.title: Argentina Says U.S. Refused to Detain a Wanted Arms Dealer
2002-06-09.abstract: Swiss police detain Bernard Lasnaud, French arms dealer, at request of Argentine government, whose interest in him arises from scandals involving former Pres Carlos Saul Menem; Argentine officials complain that their repeated requests to United States to detain Lasnaud, who lives in Florida, have been ignored for more than four years; former Justice Dept officials say exceptions to such requests are sometimes made for people who help US government (M)

2002-06-09.title: For Company A, War Is Nearly Over but Many G.I.'s See Afghan Mission as Not Quite Completed
2002-06-09.abstract: The soldiers from Alpha Company have been in Afghanistan for six months, and can now count on one page of the calendar the days before they go home.

2002-06-09.title: Korean Troops Are on Alert For World Cup
2002-06-09.abstract: Division of 10,000 South Korean troops is placed on alert in and around city of Taegu as South Korean and American athletes prepare to face each other in crucial match in World Cup soccer tournament; American military police offices are expected to patrol stadium on day of match, which many Koreans see as epic encounter in which national pride is at stake; danger of angry reaction by fans to unfavorable calls by referees or scores against Korean team is such that Pres Kim Dae Jung decides not to attend match (M)

2002-06-10.title: Korongo Journal; Amid War, 4-Legged Cash and Dreams of Tourism
2002-06-10.abstract: Residents of southern Sudan are adopting new ways of doing business, forced by 20 years of civil war that has devastated economy and left country without banking system or currency; goats are currency of choice in money-scarce Korongo, remote village in Nuba Mountains; map; photo (M)

2002-06-10.title: Wider Military Ties With India Offer U.S. Diplomatic Leverage
2002-06-10.abstract: Military cooperation between US and India has increased remarkably since September 11 terrorist attacks to level almost unimaginable for most of past three decades; is underscored by burst of army, navy and air force joint exercises, revival of American military sales and blur of high-level visits by generals and admirals; India, for decades champion of nonalignment, seeks warmer ties with US in hopes of gaining access to sophisticated military technology and help in dealing with Pakistan over disputed territory of Kashmir; US hopes deeper military and political ties will give it measure of leverage to prevent war between both countries that could lead to nuclear holocaust and play havoc with hunt for Al Qaeda terrorists in Pakistan; upcoming visit of Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld discussed; photos (M)

2002-06-10.title: 100 Are Hurt As Soccer Loss Leads to Riot In Moscow
2002-06-10.abstract: Outdoor broadcast of World Cup soccer match turns into drunken rampage outside Kremlin after Russian team loses to Japan; hundreds of rioters destroy dozens of autos and scores of storefronts and injure perhaps 100 people; 11 law enforcement officers reportedly are wounded; photo (M)

2002-06-10.title: Pressure on Arafat Builds; Israelis Make More Arrests
2002-06-10.abstract: Israeli troops and tanks continued the sweep of Ramallah, the de facto Palestinian capital in the West Bank, that began early this morning.

2002-06-10.title: For Company A, War Is Nearly Over
2002-06-10.abstract: Soldiers of US Army's Company A, which operates out of Bagram Air Base in Afghanistan, are returning home after six-month tour of duty; many look forward to reunions with families while others feel their mission is not quite complete; some comment; map; photo (M)

2002-06-11.title: Rumsfeld Sounds Optimistic Note as He Arrives in India for Talks
2002-06-11.abstract: Defense Secretary Donald H. Rumsfeld arrived in New Delhi today for meetings with senior Indian officials, sounding a hopeful note about the outlook for reduced tensions between Pakistan and India.

2002-06-11.title: Powell Says Details of a Palestinian State Will Come Soon
2002-06-11.abstract: President Bush will soon give a more specific vision for creation of a Palestinian state, Secretary of State Colin L. Powell said today.

2002-06-11.title: H.I.V. Survey in South Africa Suggests Plateau in Infections
2002-06-11.abstract: Annual survey by South Africa's Health Ministry suggests number of HIV infections have leveled off at about one-quarter of adult population; epidemiologists estimate 4.74 million people, out of population of 44 million, are currently infected--still largest number of adults of any country in world; graph (M)

2002-06-11.title: Bush Sees Sharon and Says Time Isn't Ripe for Peace Conference
2002-06-11.abstract: Pres Bush meets with Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon at White House and says time is not ripe for Mideast peace summit since 'no one' has confidence in emerging Palestinian government; comes close to endorsing Sharon's position, echoing his skepticism about conducting political talks with Palestinians until there is significant reform of Palestinian Authority; says Authority under Yasir Arafat is incapable o engaging in political talks administration has assured Arab leaders will be conducted simultaneously with security talks; suggests Arafat's reorganization of Cabinet did not solve problem of finding Palestinian partner; meeting at Camp David 48 hours earlier with Egyptian Pres Hosni Mubarak, who gave opposite advice and pressed for timeline for establishment of Palestinian state, noted; Israeli tanks surround Arafat's compound in Ramallah on West Bank; security forces arrest 27 Palestinians suspected of being militants; photo (M)

2002-06-11.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Annan Opens Food Conference
2002-06-11.abstract: Secretary General Kofi Annan opened a United Nations conference on hunger by urging a fairer distribution of food supplies and support for farmers. ''While some countries produce more than they need to feed their people,'' he said in Rome, ''others do not, and many of these cannot afford to import enough to make up the gap.'' Most speakers on the first day were African heads of state who called for removal of barriers to farm trade with developed countries and greater access by farmers in poorer nations to land, cheap loans, and technology for producing hardier crops. Mr. Annan, who is from Ghana, said that almost 200 million people in Africa, or 28 percent of the population, are chronically hungry, and that several countries in southern Africa face ''outright famine'' in coming months.   John Tagliabue (NYT)

2002-06-12.title: Manzoorabad Journal; Bondage's Load: Heavy Bricks, and Crushing Debt
2002-06-12.abstract: Manzoorabad Journal on plight of Pakistan's bonded laborers and their families, who get high-interest loans from employers, often brickmakers, and then must labor to pay off ever-mounting debt; photo; map; practice, particularly bonded child labor, is illegal but law is rarely if ever enforced; US human rights report, putting number at several million workers, says strong social ties between employers and public officials at local level undercut law (M)

2002-06-12.title: 9 More North Koreans Seek Asylum in Seoul Embassy in Beijing
2002-06-12.abstract: Nine more North Koreans sneak into South Korean diplomatic building in Beijing, bringing number of new asylum seekers to 17(S)

2002-06-12.title: Castro Calls March Against Bush's Criticism
2002-06-12.abstract: Pres Fidel Castro calls for massive marches by Cubans to protest Bush administration's hard-line policies (S)

2002-06-12.title: Palestinian Suicide Bomber Kills a Girl and Wounds 8
2002-06-12.abstract: Palestinian blows himself up in restaurant in Israeli coastal town of Herzliya, killing 15-year-old girl and wounding eight other people; photo; map; Palestinians report boy and man killed by army gunfire near Jewish settlement in Gaza; army reports three gunmen shot; Arab gunmen in Hebron kill two fellow Palestinians suspected of being informers; Israeli forces carry out fresh sweeps in Ramallah, arresting Abdel Rahim Mallouh, deputy chief of Popular Front for Liberation of Palestine (M)

2002-06-12.title: Holocaust Payments by Swiss Delayed Further by New Approach
2002-06-12.abstract: Controversy over speeding up payments to Holocaust victims through Claims Resolution Council further delays disbursement of money from $1.25-billion settlement with Swiss banks over war-era accounts; tribunal supervisor Paul A Volcker explains (S)

2002-06-13.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: Muslim Leader Arrested
2002-06-13.abstract: Egypt arrests Islamic Group co-founder Salah Hashem and another group member; Hashem had been one of main supporters of cease-fire announced by militant group (S)

2002-06-13.title: Critic of Corruption in Rural China Is Arrested in a Crackdown
2002-06-13.abstract: Li Lan, villager in China's Hunan Province who has crusaded against corruption and lawlessness in countryside, is arrested on charge of malicious slander of local officials; photo (M)

2002-06-13.title: Pakistan Holds Terror Suspects; Cites U.S. Ties
2002-06-13.abstract: Pakistan officials say several men believed to be American citizens have been taken into custody during past few weeks on suspicion of being linked to Al Qaeda; say most were picked up along with other suspected Al Qaeda and Taliban members in joint American-Pakistani raids in country's remote tribal areas near border with Afghanistan; say they believe that men form disjointed network of disaffected Westerners who converted to Islam and have been drawn to militant causes; one man, who goes by name Ahmed Muhammad or single name Benjamin and claims to be American citizen, is believed to be associate of Jose Padilla, American-born man detained last month on suspicion that he was trying to build radiation dispersal bomb intended for detonation in United States; report they have picked up about 400 suspected members of Al Qaeda and Taliban in sweeps around country since December, about 300 of whom have been turned over to American authorities; say some of men recently detained and believed to be Americans may have studied under radical Islamic cleric Mufti Muhammad Iltimas, to whom John Walker Lindh and Richard C Reid may also have had link; also say they have confirmed that unidentified American convert to Islam was killed while fighting alongside Muslim guerrillas in Kashmir; map (M)

2002-06-13.title: Voices of Afghan Democracy, Loud and Unclear
2002-06-13.abstract: First day of open debate in loya jirga, or grand council, to select Afghanistan's transitional government, is marked by raucous arguments among 1,551 delegates over wide variety of issues; more than half of agenda is postponed, including selection of head of state; photo (M)

2002-06-13.title: World Briefing | Asia: Sri Lanka: Rights Group To Meet Rebels
2002-06-13.abstract: Amnesty International representatives will meet with Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam leaders in Sri Lanka for first time to discuss their recruitment of child soldiers and need to let Muslims they have expelled return to north; will discuss with government its alleged failure to effectively prosecute soldiers and policemen who kill or rape civilians (S)

2002-06-14.title: Osaka Journal; Oh-So-Sober Japan, Suddenly Drunk on Soccer
2002-06-14.abstract: Osaka explodes in frenzy when Japan beats Russia for its first ever World Cup soccer victory, scene that was repeated all over Japan; almost no one had predicted that normally subdued populace would be swept up in wild revelry, but Japan's affection for soccer, which began tentatively decade ago, has turned into head-over-heels love affair that reflects deep-seated generational changes; map; photos (Osaka Journal) (M)

2002-06-14.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Treason Trial To Continue
2002-06-14.abstract: Russian court refuses to halt treason trial of Oleg D Kalugin, former KGB general living in United States; he is accused of disclosing state secrets from Soviet era; has refused to appear in Russia to defend himself (S)

2002-06-14.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: Number Of Executions Jumps
2002-06-14.abstract: Amnesty International reports 213 people were executed in Egypt in past decade compared with 35 in previous one; scores 21-year-old emergency law, state security courts and military trials for Islamic militants; 67 militants have been executed since 1992 (S)2

2002-06-14.title: Corrections
2002-06-14.abstract: Correction of June 13 article comparing features of four megapixel digital cameras, which misstated battery life of some models

2002-06-14.title: Corrections
2002-06-14.abstract: Correction: two picture credits with Critic's Notebook article about new international piano competition misspelled name of the photographer; she is Dawn Villella

2002-06-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Gun Law Vote
2002-06-15.abstract: Lower house of German Parliament votes to tighten gun laws; move comes on heels of school massacre on Apr 26 in city of Erfurt, where former student shot and killed 16 people, then himself; upper house is expected to ratify decision (S)

2002-06-15.title: Fighting 'Light Skin' as a Standard of Beauty
2002-06-15.abstract: Irene Njoroge, cosmetologist in Nairobi, Kenya, campaigns against skin-lightening cream, product that has become essential part of many African women's beauty routines, and big business, too; says women are being exploited by irresponsible beauty manufacturers who are cashing in on common perception in Africa that lighter one's skin, wealthier, better educated and more attractive one is; notes that labels on skin-whitening agents fail to disclose potentially harmful, sometimes fatal, effects of creams; her organizaiton, called Beauty Without Danger, conducts neighborhood tours to document extent of problem to Kenyan authorities; photo (M)

2002-06-15.title: By Aiding Needy Filipinos, G.I.'s Could Help Rout the Rebels
2002-06-15.abstract: Stated mission of 160 American Special Forces soldiers in Philippines is to train Philippine soldiers to fight dwindling band of Abu Sayyaf rebels who have operated on island of Basilan; American forces are playing equally important role in mending roads, drilling wells and clearing airstrip for cargo planes; goal is to improve life for 300,000 residents of island in one of poorest parts of country, and hopefully to help dry up popular support for Abu Sayyaf (M)

2002-06-15.title: Iceland Bars American Falun Gong Followers
2002-06-15.abstract: State Department asks Iceland's government why it ordered state airline, Icelandair, to refuse passage to American citizens who appear on list of followers of Falun Gong spiritual movement; Amb Jon Baldvin Hannibalsson of Iceland, in Washington, says his government does not want protests to interfere with visit this week of Pres Jiang Zemin of China, country that has branded Falun Gong as 'evil cult' (M)

2002-06-15.title: CAR BOMB EXPLODES OUTSIDE U.S. OFFICE IN A PAKISTANI CITY
2002-06-15.abstract: Huge car bomb explodes outside American Consulate in Karachi, Pakistan, killing at least 11 people and wounding at least 26 in most vivid demonstration yet of expanding terror campaign aimed at foreigners and government of Pres Pervez Musharraf; none of those killed are believed to be Americans, but five of those wounded worked inside heavily fortified building; police suspect bomb was set off by remote control and that occupants of car were themselves killed; this is second car-bomb attack in Karachi in less than month; level of destruction and sophistication in bombings may point to links between local militant groups and rogue members of Al Qaeda who have infiltrated Pakistan after being routed from Afghanistan; previously unknown group, Al Qanoon, claims responsibility for bombing, but investigators suspect same militants who went underground after government crackdown have regrouped and taken new name; Pres Bush calls attack work of 'radical killers' and pledges to 'hunt them down, and seek justice'; privately, Bush's aides voice concern that bombings are sign that Musharraf's power is eroding; photos; map (M)

2002-06-16.title: Reporter's Notebook; On Tour With Rumsfeld, the Jacket Stays On and the Monkeys Stay Away
2002-06-16.abstract: Reporter's Notebook gives sidelights of Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld's visit to Persian Gulf area, and Asian subcontinent (M)

2002-06-16.title: Ulster Violence Prompts a Call For Crisis Talks
2002-06-16.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair and his Irish counterpart, Bertie Ahern, order emergency talks with political parties in Northern Ireland over rising violence and deepening political distrust in province (S)

2002-06-16.title: Amid Prosperity, Toronto Shows Signs of Fraying
2002-06-16.abstract: Toronto, Canada's premier city for business and finance, is showing signs of fraying, even while Canada's economy is strong, property values are rising and housing construction is booming for rich and upper middle class; poor have been left behind in otherwise bustling housing market; city officials estimate that 6,000 people are now homeless in Toronto; photos; maps (M)

2002-06-16.title: On Old Green Line, a Fence and Fears Are Rising
2002-06-16.abstract: Israel is set to build 225-mile security fence to wall off Palestinians on West Bank from Israel proper; it is a measure of fear that what year ago might have seemed an unlikely idea is now wildly popular among Israelis, though not all of them; settlers and some right-wing Israelis oppose fence, and Palestinians are uniformly against it; photos; map (M)

2002-06-16.title: Czech Voters Block Return of Center-Right Ex-Leader
2002-06-16.abstract: Czech voters reject comeback bid by their contentious former prime minister, Vaclav Klaus, opting apparently for less confrontation in politics and smooth road toward joining European Union; voters choose to keep in power center-left Social Democrats, whose new leader, Vladimir Spidla, campaigned on greatly increased spending and quick accession to European Union; photos (M)

2002-06-17.title: BUSH TO FORMALIZE A DEFENSE POLICY OF HITTING FIRST
2002-06-17.abstract: Pres Bush reportedly directs top national security aides to make doctrine of pre-emptive action against states and terrorist groups trying to develop weapons of mass destruction into foundation of new national security strategy; policy represents significant move away from chesslike military strategies of cold war, dealing more broadly with range of options to prevent nations from obtaining large-scale weapons or sponsoring terrorism; is being fine-tuned to make it clear that US has options beyond armed intervention, such as joint operations with Russia and other powers; potential targets include weak states that have become incubators for terrorist groups; Iraq is clearly first on target list for such action; CIA and Defense Department have stepped up efforts to unseat Saddam Hussein in last effort to avoid necessity of full-scale invasion; photo (M)

2002-06-17.title: Palestinian Blows Himself Up Near Site of West Bank Fence
2002-06-17.abstract: A Palestinian suicide bomber blew himself up today in the West Bank area where Israel is building its new security fence, and two other Palestinian fighters were killed.

2002-06-17.title: Vatican City Journal; The Friar, Miraculously, Marches Into Sainthood
2002-06-17.abstract: Padre Pio, Capuchin friar who for 50 years was said to have borne bleeding wounds of Jesus and whose worldwide following blends medieval piety with modern celebrity cult, is canonized at Vatican in solemn Mass celebrated by Pope John Paul II; one of largest crowds in memory packs St Peter's Square; photos (M)

2002-06-17.title: U.S. Messages to Arab Youth, Wrapped in Song
2002-06-17.abstract: Radio Sawa, new radio station established by US government, beams news and music to youth throughout Arab world to counteract anti-American diatribes on Middle East airwaves (M)

2002-06-17.title: Mexico Begins to Wade Through Morass of Police Corruption
2002-06-17.abstract: Many Mexicans say biggest law enforcement problem is corrupt police force; efforts to correct problem, declared one of highest priorities by Pres Vicente Fox, is becoming epic struggle; even though government has made significent progress, states and cities report little measurable improvement (M)

2002-06-18.title: Israeli Forces Raid West Bank After Suicide Bomber Kills 19
2002-06-18.abstract: Israeli forces raided the West Bank late Tuesday after a Palestinian suicide bomber launched the worst attack in Jerusalem in six years.

2002-06-18.title: Bush Administration Says Two Americans Detained in Pakistan
2002-06-18.abstract: The Bush administration disclosed today that two American citizens of Afghan origin were detained in Pakistan last month.

2002-06-18.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-06-18.abstract: PAKISTAN: Ready To Match An Indian Pullback; PHILIPPINES: Gunmen Fire At U.S. Troops; CHINA: More North Koreans Seek Asylum; HONG KONG: Falun Gong Members On Trial; INDONESIA: Police Kill Separatist Rebels;.

2002-06-18.title: Center-Left Leader in Prague Is Asked to Form Government
2002-06-18.abstract: Czech Republic Pres Vaclav Havel asks center-left leader Vladimir Spidla to form government as concern grows that center-left coalition might not be strong enough to push through reforms needed to take country into European Union; Spidla's ruling Social Democrats finished first in June 14 parliamentary elections, taking 30 percent of vote and winning 70 seats in 200-seat lower house (M)

2002-06-18.title: An American's Death Bares Guatemalan Blunders
2002-06-18.abstract: Death of American journalist Larry Lee remains unsolved two-and-a-half years after he was murdered in apartment in Guatemala City, Guatemala; despite increasing pressure from US, his family in Missouri still has no idea who killed him as Guatemalan investigators have barely pressed inquiry, overlooking important evidence and letting trail to suspects go cold; killing is example of justice system in which justice is rarely done; unsolved killings of at least seven other Americans in Guatemala noted; list; photos (M)

2002-06-19.title: U.S. Links Peacekeeping to Immunity From New Court
2002-06-19.abstract: United States aide Richard S Williamson warns Security Council meeting on renewing mandate of international forces in Bosnia that Americans will not take part in United Nations peacekeeping missions unless council grants them immunity from prosecution by new International Criminal Court; diplomats do not expect US to block extension of Bosnia force, especially since it is essentially NATO operation with UN endorsement; see bid to press council, especially Britain and France, to explicity exempt UN peacekeepers from prosecution before court comes into being July 1; US efforts to deny court jurisdiction over Americans serving abroad has become symbol of what many at UN see as Bush administration challenge to idea of universal legal system (M)

2002-06-19.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: Report On Hostage Rescue Effort
2002-06-19.abstract: Philippine military reports operation to rescue two Americans and Filipino held by Muslim group Abu Sayyaf was 'meticulously' planned for over six months; American Martin Burnham and Filipino Ediborah Yap died in effort (S)

2002-06-19.title: Saudi Arabia Arrests 13 Men Tied to Attack on a U.S. Base
2002-06-19.abstract: Saudi Arabia, after denying for months that any Al Qaeda members might be lurking in kingdom, reports arresting some months ago six Saudi and one Sudanese member of terrorist network it believes tried to down United States military aircraft with shoulder-fired missile; reports arresting second group of five Saudis and an Iraqi for hiding Sudanese man, alleged ringleader, and helping him to escape to Sudan, where he was arrested and returned to Saudi Arabia (M)

2002-06-19.title: Inquiry on Canadians' Deaths Says U.S. Pilots Broke Rules
2002-06-19.abstract: Military officials say probe found Air National Guard pilot Major Harry Schmidt, who mistakenly dropped bomb on Canadian soldiers conducting nighttime live-fire exercise in Afghanistan in Apr, and pilot of second plane broke procedures by not taking time to assess threat properly; say Schmidt thought he was under attack from ground when he dropped bomb that killed four Canadians and wounded eight; say warning from radar plane that potential frendlies were in area came seconds too late; say inquiry found second pilot, Maj William Umbach, failed to exercise leadership (M)

2002-06-19.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-06-19.abstract: THE HAGUE: Delay In Milosevic'S Trial; GERMANY: Convictions In Attack On Foreigners; CZECH REPUBLIC: Agreement On New Government; FRANCE: JuppÉ Rises On Chirac'S Coattails;.

2002-06-20.title: 2 Boys Charged With Setting Fatal Fire at a Beijing Internet Cafe
2002-06-20.abstract: Two teenage boys have been arrested and charged with intentionally setting the fire that killed 24 last weekend in an unlicensed Internet cafe.

2002-06-20.title: Russia Seeking to Dismantle Collective Farms
2002-06-20.abstract: Russia is moving to dismantle collectivization of its farmlands; Parliament's lower house, urged by Pres Vladimir V Putin, has approved preliminary bill to create legal system to buy and sell agricultural lands and is expected to adopt slightly amended version; Communist and Agrarian parties, regional leaders and privatization opponents are threatening to block effort; collectives still run over 90 percent of farms; farmers fear repeat of earlier corrupted privatizations that gave control of former state enterprises to richest oligarchs; map; photo (M)

2002-06-20.title: When Asylum Seekers Knock, Europe Is Deaf
2002-06-20.abstract: Asylum seekers are finding Europe has grown deaf to their plight; European Union leaders are set to meet to discuss steps to tighten borders to combat illegal immigration amid rising anti-immigrant sentiment; new Danish laws that make it clear immigrants are no longer welcome in nation once proud of its openness cited; politicians across Europe are under pressure from voters to adopt policies at least as tough as neighbors'; photo (M)

2002-06-20.title: Bush Offers Plan to Help Mothers Avoid Passing H.I.V. to Babies
2002-06-20.abstract: Pres Bush announces $500 million plan to prevent pregnant women from passing AIDS virus to their babies and reduce number of infected babies by 40 percent in dozen African nations and Caribbean; new money, which includes $200 million already set aside by Congress, will provide treatment to million women a year over five years; reaction among advocates for people with AIDS is mixed, with some praising Bush and others holding plan falls short of global needs (M)

2002-06-20.title: JERUSALEM BLAST KILLS SIX ISRAELIS; ARMY RAIDS START
2002-06-20.abstract: Second Palestinian suicide bomber strikes Jerusalem, killing six people at bus stop, even as Israel implements policy of taking back West Bank land in retaliation for attacks; Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat condemns both bombings; new bombing apparently spurs series of Israeli military actions, reportedly including helicopter gunship attacks in Gaza Strip and fierce firefight in Qalqilya; Israel, responding to first bombing, moved trailer houses into Jenin, shut city down in curfew and rounded up hundreds of Palestinians; map; photo (M)

2002-06-21.title: More Tears in a Land of Mourning
2002-06-21.abstract: Noa Alon, 60, and her granddaughter Gal, five, killed on Israeli bus by Palestinian suicide bomber, are buried together; photos; retired teacher had returned to direct year-end kindergarten concert in which Gal performed; child's mother and baby brother were injured in attack that took seven lives (S)

2002-06-21.title: Political appointees to replace 14 senior civil servants : Hong Kong realigns government
2002-06-21.abstract: HONG KONG:—  Hong Kong legislators have voted to approve the most sweeping changes to the territory's government structure since China resumed sovereignty over the former British colony five years ago.

2002-06-21.title: Desire for Sons Drives Use of Prenatal Scans in China
2002-06-21.abstract: China's villagers are using inexpensive prenatal scans and then abortion in greater numbers than ever to prevent birth of unwanted daughters and ensure they will have sons; recent studies and census data show gap between male and female births has only widened in last decade, giving China largest gender disparity in world; imbalance in some rural areas is staggering 144 boys for every 100 girls born; China's overall figure is 117 to 100, compared with 105 to 100 in US; photo; map; graph; example is southeastern region of Chaoyang, where daughters move away to husband's families, government no longer provides even pretense of old-age or medical care, and people depend on sons (M)

2002-06-21.title: Germany Eases Restrictions On Workers From Abroad
2002-06-21.abstract: German Pres Johannes Rau signs immigration law that significantly expands residence permits available to workers from countries outside the European Union, at a time when countries across Continent are moving toward tightening borders (S)

2002-06-21.title: Prosecution Completes Case in Trial of Slain Reporter
2002-06-21.abstract: The man accused of organizing the kidnapping and slaying of Daniel Pearl told a court today that the case against him was a jumble of lies.

2002-06-22.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Europe Declared Free Of Polio
2002-06-22.abstract: World Health Orgn declares Europe free of polio (S)

2002-06-22.title: Dangerous locations : Security risks often underrated
2002-06-22.abstract: On moving to Paris, personal safety was the least of my concerns — I considered myself city savvy having lived in London for five years. Had I known that I would be sexually assaulted on a Paris Metro platform, I might have been a little less cavalier.

2002-06-22.title: Defendant in Pearl's Death Says Evidence Was Faked
2002-06-22.abstract: Ahmed Omar Sheikh, man accused of organizing kidnapping and murder of Wall Street Journal reporter Daniel Pearl in Pakistan, tells court in Karachi that case against him is jumble of lies and faked evidence concocted by government to please United States; says he never met Pearl, was in another city when he was kidnapped and had nothing to do with menacing e-mail messages that threatened reporter's death; Sheikh faces possible death sentence, along with three men who are accused of helping him send e-mail messages; authorities say they have detained six more men with possible connections to case since exhuming body believed to be that of Pearl (M)

2002-06-22.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Antiradiation Pills For Everyone
2002-06-22.abstract: Government of Ireland sends potassium iodide tablets to households across country for use in case Ireland is exposed to radioactive fallout from terror attack or accident at British nuclear reprocessing plant at Sellafield, across Irish Sea (S)

2002-06-22.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-06-22.abstract: YUGOSLAVIA: Ex-Tv Boss Jailed Over Nato Bombing; GERMANY: Antiviolence Measures; RUSSIA: Treason Trial Concludes; IRELAND: Antiradiation Pills For Everyone.

2002-06-23.title: No Taboos at Swiss Expo, Where Even Money Gets Shredded
2002-06-23.abstract: Expo.02, which is set at four lakeside locations in Switzerland, encourages Swiss to see themselves differently and pokes a little fun at some of country's foibles; two-thirds of country's 7.2 million people say they plan to attend (M)

2002-06-23.title: Chrétien Is Hoping to Aid Africa by Mobilizing Rich Nations
2002-06-23.abstract: Prime Min Jean Chretien of Canada will press other Group of 8 leaders at coming meeting in Kananaskis to forge new strategy to assist Africa; will argue that wealthiest nations should increase their aid budgets for education, agriculture and fresh water, as well as helping to restructure government and legal institutions, combat AIDS and malaria, and encourage foreign investment in Africa; will also argue for dropping of trade barriers to African textiles, footwear and farm products (M)

2002-06-23.title: Meager Harvests in Africa Leave Millions at the Edge of Starvation
2002-06-23.abstract: Severe hunger is sweeping across southern Africa as result of two years of alternating droughts and floods, coupled with mismanagement of food supplies; Malawi, Zimbabwe, Zambia and Lesotho have been declared national disasters, and Mozambique and Swaziland are also struggling; United Nations expects four million people to need emergency aid in next few months as this season's meager harvest runs out; photos; map; graphs (L)

2002-06-23.title: U.N. Finds AIDS Knowledge Still Lags in Stricken Nations
2002-06-23.abstract: United Nations reports that most people in developing nations have now heard of AIDS but that significant number have only limited knowledge of how to avoid it; says most people surveyed in more than three dozen countries believe AIDS cannot strike them and, as a result, are not changing their sexual habits enough to meet threat; says there is relatively low incidence of AIDS in northern Africa, Middle East and parts of Asia with conservative Muslim populations with strong taboos about sexual behavior (M)

2002-06-23.title: Israeli Military Advances Into More of the West Bank; Plans a 'Crushing' Response
2002-06-23.abstract: Israeli ground forces move into West Bank village of Dura after Israeli security cabinent endorses plan to seize and hold Palestinian-controlled territory in response to Palestinian attacks that have killed 33 Israelis in past week; army is now holding positions in at least five of eight major Palestinian cities and towns in West Bank; photo; map (M)

2002-06-24.title: Chile Will Privatize a New Span of Its Noted Social Safety Net
2002-06-24.abstract: Chile's nominally Socialist government will allow private sector to manage nation's unemployment insurance system, which will rely on market forces (M)

2002-06-24.title: Hong Kong fashions, long known for glamour, turn casual
2002-06-24.abstract: HONG KONG:—  Lily Cheung, 24, spoke for herself and her boyfriend as they trawled the stores at the Pacific Place shopping mall, wearing skinny fit jeans with loose tops and searching for a bargain in the summer sales.

2002-06-24.title: North Korean Asylum Seekers Leave China
2002-06-24.abstract: China permits 26 North Korean asylum seekers who have sought refuge in foreign diplomatic compounds in Beijing in last month to leave its territory for South Korea, ending weeks of diplomatic gridlock; is bound by treaty with old ally North Korea to repatriate asylum seekers, but has come under intense pressure by new allies South Korea and US, who vehemently and vocally oppose repatriation (M)

2002-06-24.title: G.I.'s Fight Afghan Devastation With Plaster and Nails
2002-06-24.abstract: American soldiers in Kunduz, Afghanistan, are on front lines of effort to rebuild country's infrastructure; are working with several other civil affairs teams, local officials and warlords to reconstruct schools, hospitals, roads and water systems ruined by two decades of war; map; photo (M)

2002-06-24.title: Cambodia-listed ship was carrying cocaine : Raid at sea highlights flag abuses
2002-06-24.abstract: PHNOM PENH:—  When French commandos boarded the Cambodian-registered freighter Winner in international waters in the Atlantic this month amid an exchange of gunfire that wounded one of the 12 crew members, they seized more than a ton of cocaine in a cargo that was registered as scrap iron destined for Bilbao, Spain.

2002-06-25.title: President's Speech Is Criticized For Lacking Specific Proposals
2002-06-25.abstract: Arab world, hoping for detailed American proposal for peace and Palestinian state, finds Pres Bush's speech demanding ouster of Yasir Arafat as Palestinian leader before US can back establishment of Palestinian state short on specific timetable and long on demands for Palestinian reform; few Arabs believe Bush speech went far enough in offering kind of incentives needed to stem risisng violence and death toll on both sides (S)

2002-06-25.title: Victim Links Retired General To Torture in El Salvador War
2002-06-25.abstract: Trial of two retired Salvadoran generals in two-decade-old torture case opens in West Palm Beach, Fla, with witness singling out one as participant in brutal 24-day interrogation; Dr Juan Romagoza identifies Gen Carlos Eugenio Vides Casanova as being present during weeks in which he was tortured by members of El Salvador's military; federal civil suit against Vides and Gen Jose Guillermo Garcia, who have lived in USsince 1989, is second they have faced; were cleared of similar charges in October 2000 suit brought by relatives of four American church women who were raped and murdered by Salvadoran military in 1980 (M)

2002-06-25.title: With a Quebec Law, Equality for Gay Parents
2002-06-25.abstract: New Quebec provincial law, which takes effect at end of June, will give gay and lesbian couples same full parental rights and obligations extended to heterosexual couples; is heralded by Canadian gay rights activists as most important advance for their cause since Netherlands in 2001 granted gay couples right to marry; is seen as another sign that rapid social change continues to sweep province that two generations ago was largely directed by Roman Catholic church; its provisions discussed; photo (M)

2002-06-25.title: Artisanal roots nurture industry built on integration : It all starts with the best textiles
2002-06-25.abstract: MILAN—  Highly fragmented, intensely specialized and still largely owned by the same families that started the businesses, Italy's textiles industry remains the most important component in fashion worldwide, supplying top designers with quality materials. But its continuing success may depend on whether it can resist the modern business mantra that says market economics, consolidation and vertical integration are essential to survive in a global marketplace.

2002-06-25.title: World Briefing: Asia
2002-06-25.abstract: INDIA: A Warning To Pakistan; INDIA: Support For Embattled Neighbor; HONG KONG: Leader Appoints New Team; KAZAKHSTAN: Law On Political Parties;.

2002-06-26.title: Afghan Women in Political Spotlight
2002-06-26.abstract: Afghanistan's women are determinedly entering traditional male domain of politics, moving quickly out of invisible status under Taliban rule and into visible, activist role; 200 women joined 1,400 male delegates at loya jirga and used that platform to tell Afghanistan that women want peace and reconstruction of their country; still, many outspoken women say they feel strong undertow of resistance to their return to public life and wonder how far women have to go to win equal rights; photos of Dr Sima Samar, elected deputy chairwoman of loya jirga, and Dr Massouda Jalal, first woman to be presidential candidate (M)

2002-06-26.title: C.I.A. Instructs Spy Agencies to Use More Commercial Satellite Photos
2002-06-26.abstract: Central Intelligence Agency Director George J Tenet has ordered American intelligence agencies to expand their use of satellite photography provided by private companies, freeing government's own satellites for more specialized and secretive work; order follows complaints from Air Force officials that bureaucratic tangles initially prevented American pilots from using advanced commercial satellite imagery for maps in bombing missions (M)

2002-06-26.title: World Briefing | Europe: Mont Blanc Tunnel Reopens
2002-06-26.abstract: Mont Blanc tunnel connecting France and Italy, closed three years ago after truck fire killed 39 people, reopens despite protests by thousands of residents and environmentalists; map (S)

2002-06-26.title: Malaysia's Prime Minister to Step Down After 2 Decades
2002-06-26.abstract: Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad of Malaysia says he will step down on Oct 25, 2003, after two decades in power; statement ends short drama that began when he abruptly announced few days ago that he was resigning, but was then convinced by party leaders to retract his statement; chooses Abdullah Ahmad Badawi, deputy prime minister, as his successor; photo

2002-06-26.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iran: U.S. Aid Offer Accepted
2002-06-26.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami says Iran will accept United States offer of aid for survivors of earthquake in northwestern section of country; offer was made through Switzerland since US and Iran do not have diplomatic relations (S)

2002-06-27.title: U.S. Defends Its Farm Subsidies Against Rising Foreign Criticism
2002-06-27.abstract: Bush administration's top trade and agricultural officials respond to rising chorus of foreign criticism of new American farm subsidies, dismissing complaints as unfounded or hypocritical attempts to maintain trade barriers (M)

2002-06-27.title: World Briefing: Europe
2002-06-27.abstract: BELGIUM: Sharon War-Crimes Suit Dismissed; THE HAGUE: Skepticism About Milosevic; NORTHERN IRELAND: Effort To Ease Tensions; RUSSIA: Another Step Toward Private Farmland

2002-06-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: The Hague: Skepticism About Milosevic
2002-06-27.abstract: Judges trying Slobodan Milosevic on war-crimes charges order thorough medical checkup of Milosevic, whose claims of illness have forced delays in hearings and taken up time allotted to prosecution's case (S)

2002-06-27.title: Palestinian Authority Sets a January Vote
2002-06-27.abstract: Palestinian Authority officially announce plans to hold elections for president and legislative offices in January as part of broad proposal for civic and security reform that stops short of Pres Bush's demand for Yasir Arafat's replacement; one Palestinian minister says Arafat will run again, while others say it is too early to predict who candidates will be; Hussam Khader, Palestinian legislators and one of Arafat's most passionate critics, says Bush's demand for Arafat's removal will keep him in power for years to come; photo (M)

2002-06-27.title: Palestinian Resistance at Hebron Offices Thins Out, but Army Standoff Continues
2002-06-27.abstract: Israeli military keeps up pressure on West Bank, firing thousands of rounds into old British police station in Hebron in standoff with suspected Palestinian militants that has ground on now for two days; 130 people emerge from building, and 13 of them are arrested as terror suspects; Hebron standoff is one of few instances of violent resistance to Israel's new major military operations in West Bank; photo; graph of death toll in violence that began Sept 29, 2000: at least 550 Israelis and 1,600 Palestinians (M)

2002-06-28.title: World Briefing: Americas
2002-06-28.abstract: ARGENTINA: Strike After Death Of Protesters; CUBA: Socialism Forever, Assembly Declares;.

2002-06-28.title: U.N. Publicly Chastises China for Inaction on H.I.V. Epidemic
2002-06-28.abstract: UN issues stinging public criticism of China's lackluster efforts to face its rapidly accelerating epidemic of HIV infection and AIDS, saying country is on 'verge of a catastrophe' and could have largest number of infected people in world in few years; Science article makes same point; China puts number of infections at 800,000-1.5 million at end of 2001, but UN officials say privately there could be as many as 6 million cases already (S)

2002-06-28.title: Israeli Forces Hit Palestinian Offices in Hebron in Effort to Dislodge Gunmen
2002-06-28.abstract: Israeli troops pound Palestinian Authority offices in Hebron for third day, firing heavy machine guns and rockets from helicopters to dislodge 15 gunmen holed up inside; seven Palestinian cities remain shut down for fifth day; two Palestinians killed in clashes; Israeli troops storm Nablus jail used as security headquarters, taking about 20 Palestinians into custody; current action is direct response to attacks that killed 31 Israelis in three days; Israeli prosecutors charge Lebanese-born Israeli, son of Jewish mother and Muslim father, with spying for Hezbollah (S)

2002-06-28.title: World Briefing | Americas: Cuba: Socialism Forever, Assembly Declares
2002-06-28.abstract: Vowing never to return to capitalism, the National Assembly has voted unanimously that the country's socialist system is ''irrevocable.'' The vote Wednesday night was seen by opposition leaders as an effort to block a dissident movement calling for increased personal and political freedoms. President Fidel Castro also warned that he might cut Cuba's limited relations with the United States if Washington continued to support dissidents and human rights advocates on the island. American diplomats in Havana recently distributed hundreds of short-wave radios able to receive Radio Martí, the anti-Castro station financed by the United States.   David Gonzalez (NYT)

2002-06-28.title: China Emerges As Rival to U.S. In Asian Trade
2002-06-28.abstract: China is rapidly strenthening economic presence across Asia, gobbling up foreign investment and chipping away at US position as region's economic engine, while assuring countries that it is offering cooperation, not competition; many countries have rushed to China's embrace, nimbly shifting economic alliances at time when US is making its own way through tentative economic recovery; analysts say China's thrust, more apparent with new WTO membership, begins inescapable process of replacing US as dominant power in Asia; note that China is already economic and political threat to Japan; examples include Indonesia, where Pres Megawati Sukarnoputri has buried age-old hostilities in hopes of getting $9 billion liquid natural gas contract to power industries of southern China; China is also grabbing much of new foreign investment in Asia, leaving once-glittering Thailand, South Korea and Singapore with but crumbs; graphs showing rising Chinese share of ASEAN trade (M)

2002-06-29.title: World Briefing | Americas: Guatemala: Fears Over Dengue Fever
2002-06-29.abstract: Guatemalan health officials declare national alert against dengue fever after tallying 789 cases, Jan-May; urge people to clean homes to stop breeding mosquitoes that carry potentially fatal disease (S)

2002-06-29.title: German Leader Hopes Soccer Will Lift His Standing in Polls
2002-06-29.abstract: German Chancellor Gerhard Schroder, concerned that his party continues to trail in polls behind conservative opponents led by Edmund Stoiber, seeks to associate himself with Germany's unexpectedly good showing in World Cup soccer championships; travels on Japanese Prime Min Junichiro Koizumi's plane from Group of 8 summit meeting in Canada to see Germans play Brazil in final, Yokohama; Germany has also chartered plane to take senior leaders and politicians to game; Stoiber will attend game and return home with team; Schroder is also trying to take initiative on more substantive issues; photo (M)

2002-06-29.title: Canadian Press Freedom Questioned in Publisher's Firing
2002-06-29.abstract: Canadian news organizations are not known for digging deeply into the closets of politicians to look for skeletons.    So eyebrows were raised earlier this month when The Ottawa Citizen, the capital's most prominent newspaper, published a scathing article accusing Prime Minister Jean Chrétien of unethical conduct in his business dealings. The article appeared the same day the newspaper ran an editorial calling on Mr. Chrétien to resign.

2002-06-29.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Sharp Rise In Violence
2002-06-29.abstract: Police report deaths and injuries from guerrilla activity rose sharply in Northern Ireland in 2001 despite cease-fires by paramilitary groups; say most of 18 killings were by Protestant paramilitary groups, while Catholic and Protestant gangs were equally responsible for over 300 beatings by vigilante enforcers (S)

2002-06-29.title: Talks in Mexico Push Regional Growth
2002-06-29.abstract: The presidents of Mexico and Central America today reiterated their commitment to a regional development plan aimed at stimulating their struggling economies as they ended a two-day summit meeting in the southern Mexican state of Yucatán.     The plan, proposed a year ago by President Vicente Fox of Mexico, calls on the leaders to commit public funds and to pursue private financing to build roads, airports, railways and energy installations across a region that is largely cut off from the advances of globalization. Plan Puebla-Panama is named for the new economic bloc that extends from the central Mexican state of Puebla to Panama.

2002-06-30.title: Nuclear Dump Disrupts a Peaceful Taiwan Island
2002-06-30.abstract: People of Lan Yu, small Taiwanese island, threaten to take drastic action unless government lives up to promise made 12 years ago to remove as many as 20,000 barrels of radioactive debris from Taiwan's nuclear reactor from island; barrels, which are cracking and coming apartment because of chemical reactions inside, are in seaside concrete trenches vulnerable to typhoons, earthquakes and rockslides; island's 3,000 people belong to Tao tribe, descended from Polynesian explorers; photo; map (M)

2002-06-30.title: A Surprising Crackdown in Malawi
2002-06-30.abstract: Pres Bakili Muluzi of Malawi defends ban on political demonstrations and the issue that prompted them: constitutional amendment to eliminate presidential term limits and allow him to prolong his rule; church leaders, lawyers and university students say Muluzi, whom they revered for ending 30 years of dictatorship and expanding political freedom, has become biggest threat to nation's fledgling democracy; photo; map (M)

2002-06-30.title: With Time Running Out, Bush Shifted Mideast Policy
2002-06-30.abstract: Pres Bush's decision to condition support for Palestinian state on removal of Yasir Arafat as its leader came late in drafting of policy speech and was influenced by last-minute Israeli intelligence report that Arafat was tied to another transfer of funds to members of Al Aksa Martyrs Brigades, which claimed responsibillity for June 19 suicide bombing; Arab leaders and Mideast experts see new position as stalling tactic, putting US policy on hold while Arafat remains defiantly in place; Arab diplomats, who have praised Bush's commitment to Palestinian statehood and who have no illusions about Arafat's flaws, privately fear that months of American disengagement will only incite region's partisans to more violence (M)

2002-06-30.title: How Bush Hardened His Tone on Mideast
2002-06-30.abstract: The writing of President Bush's speech asking the Palestinians to replace their leadership went through two dozen drafts before Mr. Bush expressed some irritation, saying he was being ''nuanced to death,'' as one adviser put it. Mr. Bush asked for a clear statement of whether Washington's disappointment with Yasir Arafat had grown so deep that the peace effort could not proceed while he held power.

2002-06-30.title: Small Arms Proliferating, Study Finds
2002-06-30.abstract: Graduate Institute of International Studies in Geneva reports that recorded number of small arms around the world rose 16 percent in 2001, to 639 million; says number of such weapons, including handguns and rifles, owned by civilians rose by 25 percent, to 377 million; says US and Russia dominate manufacture of small arms (S)
